--- SOURCE 1: Current Trends in Competency Based Education ---
URL: https://www.scirp.org/journal/paperinformation?paperid=71340

SUMMARY:
The main up to date CBE practices are: 1) The number of transversal competencies vary from 6 to 10 , 2) The generic competencies most often developed are: effective communication, critical thinking, lifelong learning, cultural knowledge, problem solving, global perspective, ethics, decision making, diversity, team

FULL CONTENT:
![](../images/SRP_logo.svg)
![](../images/Open-Access.svg)

Abstract

The shortage of individuals with the abilities to perform a job position successfully demands innovative educational approaches. Competency Based Education (CBE) is a methodology that focuses on endowing students with the skills demanded by the in-dustries and evaluates them regarding what they are able to do. A bank of competen-cies that students must acquire in a university academic program was discussed. Occupational trends as well as associated skills demands were detected. This research could be a source of practical knowledge for those organizations seeking to understand CBE foundations and for those wishing to initiate their path through this global educational trend.

Keywords

[Competency Model](articles?searchcode=Competency+Model&searchfield=keyword&page=1),  [Competency Assessment](articles?searchcode=+Competency+Assessment&searchfield=keyword&page=1),  [Learning Methods](articles?searchcode=+Learning+Methods&searchfield=keyword&page=1),  [Occupational Trends](articles?searchcode=++Occupational+Trends&searchfield=keyword&page=1)

Share and Cite:

1. Introduction

Currently, workforce leaders are highlighting the importance of preparing students with the abilities demanded and recognized by the industries [1]. This issue matters to both organizations and academic institutions. The former seeks to find persons with the right knowledge and competencies for developing a job position for two main reasons: improve organizations’ performance and increase the satisfaction and the retention of employees [2]. The latter, looks to prepare professionals with high possibilities of being hired, as nowadays education is seen as an investment that must have a return [3]. In the US, only 11% of business leaders are strongly agree that students have the right skills to do well in a labor environment [4]. To solve this problem, a proper Competency Based Education (CBE) model for new professionals could contribute to the cause.

It is essential that students acquire not only the specialized knowledge, but also the needed skills to apply it and succeed in a highly demanding and ever changing world [5]. This would be the main contribution of universities, the education of individuals with the right competencies to contribute to the present and future labor environment [6].

CBE is considered by academics as an option to face these challenges [1]; it is defined as an educational system or program that measures knowledge, skills, and experience in place of or in addition to the use of credit hours [7]. The purpose is to make a beginner, in this case a student, becomes an expert in a specific field [8].

The main characteristics of CBE are: an emphasis on results, a focus on development of abilities, the demonstration of competency mastery rather than time based teaching and the student as a learning unit [9]. This scheme of education focuses on evaluate students regarding what they are able to do for the society [10].

CBE approach is not new; disciplines such as medicine, psychology and engineering have already a history in the field [11]. Organizations such as Alverno College, Excelsior College and Thomas Edison State College developed CBE programs in the 1970s [12]. Today, it has evolved and many universities and colleges have joined this initiative [7] [13].

This educational method, which has proven to be successful as there are more than 52 programs just in the US already using different variants [7], is considered a disruptive innovation that will continue revolutionizing Higher Education [4]. Current courses under CBE offer flexibility and allow students to learn at their own pace. The individuals can assess the acquisition of a competency when they are feeling ready. New cost models have been developed [1] [7]. CBE is not a trend, it has arisen from the needs of the society with the main objective of give additional value to both students and organizations [5]. It is seen as the missing link between learning outcomes and industry needs [4].

The outline of this paper is as follows: in Section 2 introduces the concept of competency. Section 3 presents a model of the basic competencies for students. Section 4 shows the CBE and occupational trends. Finally, Section 5concludes the paper.

2. Competency: An Overview

The first step for implementing a CBE initiative is to understand what a competency is. This term has a variety of definitions [2] in both educational and business context. There is lack of consensus among researchers regarding the different aspects constituting this word [12]. It is considered multidimensional, dynamic and with a developing identity, strongly influenced by the context in which it is used [14].

The UN General Secretary from 1997 to 2006, Kofi Annan, defines competency as a combination of skills, attributes and behaviors that are directly related to successful performance on the job [15]. In an educational framework, a competency will specify what students can do after completing a program. The focus is on the performance and the demonstration of their abilities [11].

An own definition of competency is: A student’s competency contains three elements: knowledge, skills and attitudes/values. These components are integrated to perform a specific activity, with measurable outcomes, that clearly indicate what the student is able to do. A competency comprises a set of resources and talents an individual has to perform a particular task.

Competencies can be categorized as disciplinary or transversal. The former, as the name suggests, comprises those competencies that are particular of a discipline. The latter, which are also known as generics, are those competencies common to the different fields of study, e.g. critical thinking, effective communication, ethics, etc. [8], and facilitates the acquisition of the other ones. In some cases, a single course can contain a mix of the above competencies.

The way graduates are taught by Higher Education Institutions (HEI) has a big influence on developing significant competencies. When the aim is to develop the aforementioned abilities, the graduates learn by doing, using and interacting [6].

CBE requires specific and nontraditional teaching methods [11]. Repetition and memorization of information do not allow students to understand the challenges and problems of this century. It is advisable to teach with learning experiences that stimulate them in developing the skills demanded by the industrial market [15].

The CBE teaching methods should allow students to be self-directed learners, practice what is taught and receive training activities that matches with their learning style [11]. These activities require continuous practice, as the acquirement of competencies is an ongoing process; this will ensure that the new skills are strengthened and will move to the long term memory. Competency development in students is an incremental process [8].

Competency development is a function of the personal attitudes of the students plus the resources provided by the HEI, such as an effective teachers mentoring [6]. Using techniques and resources to promote competencies such as: minutes writes, presentations, case studies, think-pair-share, socratic questioning, debates, notes exchange, online assignments, lectures for initial competencies training, videos, laboratories, field works, individual or group projects, expert talks, in-class group discussions, role playing, online forums, games, participation in conferences as speaker, organize seminars, field trips and creative, cultural and volunteer activities. Besides, social media is a nontraditional source to learn new capabilities. A student could follow industry leaders to identify what they are reading or obtain time management tips. He could also search for infographics to get an idea on how to improve data presentation and make it more appealing [16].

One of the main characteristics of CBE is the continuous evaluation required to verify that the competencies are being developed [11]. A person is competent on a task, by seeing her performs it [8]. CBE assessment is perhaps more challenging than the traditional model; it requires gathering and analyzing diverse information, testing the student repeatedly and offering a personalized attention, which demands significant time [17].

Evaluation of competencies allows to determine the level of acquisition, encourages learners to work to achieve the goals, develops self-correction and continuous learning abilities, and modifies or improves the strategies based on the results [18].

CBE assessment methods include: published papers, tests, projects, scoring rubrics [11], metacognitive reflection, portfolios [19], learning journals, self-appraisal, peer evaluation [8] and Behavioral Observation Scales (BOS) [18]. Software to track competencies are gaining momentum [20]. There are available tests to measure learning outcomes in a more accurate manner, such as, Comprehensive Assessment of Team Member Effectiveness, Leadership Q-sort Test and Leadership Ability Test, Test of Oral Communication Skills, California Critical Thinking Skills Test, Graduate Record Examination test, Test of English as a Foreign Language, and General Innovation Skills Aptitude Test.

Organizations with needs of specialized resource devote important efforts in their selection processes to attract the best candidates, those that will make important contributions to the business, organizations use formal assessment methods [21] such as Cognitive Ability Tests, Job Knowledge Tests, and Personality Tests.

3. Basic Competencies

The important transversal competencies were grouped in four categories based on similarities related to the expected outcomes in the performance of the students:

1) Personal performance: contains competencies will aid students to have a major impact in any context in which he works, such as, cross-cultural communication, effective communication, self-taught, world knowledge, personal management, crisis management, leadership, team-working, and networking.

2) Ethics and legality competencies will help students to proceed with integrity and within the law, such as, humanism and civic engagement, legal, sustainability, professionalism and ethical practice.

3) Supporting competencies will improve the job performance, such as, information technology, technology, argument construction, entrepreneurship, and virtual competency.

4) Intellectual competencies will ensure the work will be done correctly and with excellence, such as, critical thinking, creativity, innovation, constructing knowledge, research, decision making, problem solving, analysis capacity, and intellectual risk.

The development and adoption of CBE has been evolutionary and revolutionary. It was originated by pioneering countries such as United States, Australia and United Kingdom. Nowadays, Finland, Canada, New Zealand, Sweden, Singapore and China are some of the nations that have implemented it with major achievements. There is no consensus about the number of universities that have applied CBE to their degree programs. The main reason could be that, to date, there is no agreement about what is exactly this approach; besides, the quantity of providers is increasing rapidly [7]. Main discussions in CBE focuses on programs in which students learn content and, at the same time, develop the competencies. The credits are earn once they demonstrate the dominance of both issues. Alverno College follows the traditional system in which students learn the new material and do not move to the next one until they have understood the content. They must demonstrate the acquisition of competencies only before graduation. In Western Governors University, students earn the class credits as long as they master the competencies; learners take the assessments when they feeling ready [7]. Universities are finding difficulties in adopting a full CBE approach. They have different levels of implementation based on the availability of resources and the positioning/transitioning strategies [22].

4. Trends

The main up to date CBE practices are: 1) The number of transversal competencies vary from 6 to 10, 2) The generic competencies most often developed are: effective communication, critical thinking, lifelong learning, cultural knowledge, problem solving, global perspective, ethics, decision making, diversity, team working and technology literacy, 3) The majority of organizations offer online learning besides on campus traditional format; the blended option is also available, 4) Teaching methods are focused on experiential learning, 5) Competencies are taught in two forms: in specific courses, and embedded in the curriculum, 6) Assessment methods used to analyze if the student has achieved the learning objectives include standard tests, assignment grades, portfolio, rubrics based on evidences, observation of the learner behavior and student self- assessment, and 7) In some cases, achievement of competencies are reported with a pass or not pass grade.

New demanded professions have been developed in the last ten years. It is estimated that 75% of children entering in primary school will have jobs that today do not even exist. It is probable that more than one third of the current desired skills by employers will be replaced by abilities not yet considered as important [23].

To detect in advance the needed competencies of the future labor market, it is necessary to be aware of what is going on globally and what is expected to occur. Six of these expected changes are\_1) Technological advances will lead to a big amount of data that must be systematized to find patterns and take advantage of this knowledge, 2) There is an increment of collaboration between organizations for innovation purposes [24], 3) 36% of jobs across all industries will demand complex problem solving skills, 4) It is expected a shift in the way of working, becoming more interconnected and network oriented [24], 5) Labor environments will become multicultural, due to globalization, and multigenerational, four generations working together [24], and 6) Organizations must be able to adapt fast to new market demands and customer needs that result from changing environments.

5. Conclusion

CBE is gaining space rapidly in the global educational market. It seems it has had an organic growth as it aids to solve one important challenge: the shortage of individuals with the competencies demanded by current and future industries. This learning approach measures the knowledge, skills and experience the students acquire during a training period. It is focused on results. It ranges from formats in which competencies are taught and evaluated apart from the curriculum, in specific courses, to those in which, to gain the degree, the student must demonstrate solely the know-how acquired. The competencies that universities must develop are diverse and depend on the needs of the different economic sectors. Some of the generic competencies to promote are: effective communication in different languages, team working, ethical practice, humanism and civic engagement, virtual competency, information technology, critical thinking and problem solving. Learning activities are more experientials and require that the students have a deep involvement. Evaluation is more personalized and, in some cases, somewhat subjective, depending on the appreciation of the mentor. Many universities use standard tests and a mix of assessment methods with the aim of obtain more accurate results. The future looks promising to CBE since it is based in the needs of a fast changing society.

Conflicts of Interest

The authors declare no conflicts of interest.

--- SOURCE 2: Competency-Based Education: Transforming Modern Learning ---
URL: https://medium.com/@aileenscott604/competency-based-education-transforming-modern-learning-2cfd3472f72d

SUMMARY:
With the rising need for more flexible and competency-based education, CBE will be a central feature of the educational landscape in the future.

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fill:64:64/1*dmbNkD5D-u45r44go_cf0g.png)

# Competency-Based Education: Transforming Modern Learning

![Aileen Scott](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fill:64:64/0*iwoiM3y-jkT8TEOs.)

--

Listen

Share

![]()

The higher education sector is changing fast due to global demand, rapid technology changes, and changing learner needs. Traditional methods can be limited in their ability to equip students for real-world issues. The competency-based education (CBE) is an innovative model that places mastery of competencies ahead of time spent in a classroom. This blog explores how CBE is changing the way we learn, its impact on students, and what makes it a game-changer for any education system in today’s world. We will also explore real-world insight into the strength and relevance of CBE today.

# What is Competency-Based Education?

[***Competency-based education (CBE)***](https://www.academikamerica.com/blog/competency-based-learning-for-k12-a-comprehensive-guide) prioritizes students achieving expertise in particular skills or clusters of knowledge over enforcing rigid timelines. Instead of measuring progress by how much time someone has spent in a lesson or following a traditional grading system, learners progress when they demonstrate their competencies against defined outcomes. With individualized pacing and quantifiable progress in terms of learning, CBE meets the needs of diverse learners, whether they are working professionals, students or others looking to change their careers.

## Key Features of CBE

# Why Competency-Based Education Is Relevant Today?

Globally, skills gaps are widening. Now, the [***World Economic Forum predicts that there will be over 78 million jobs that need to be upskilled by the year 2030***](https://hr.economictimes.indiatimes.com/news/industry/urgent-upskilling-needed-to-cater-78-million-new-job-opportunities-by-2030-wef-report/117047749#:~:text=3%20min%20read-,Urgent%20upskilling%20needed%20to%20cater%2078%20million%20new%20job%20opportuniti)***.*** The same gap exists in the skill sets that companies, companies of all segments and employee ages, need from graduates of traditional education, which falls short on addressing this need. Here, CBE meets this challenge by equipping students with tangible, measurable skills they can immediately apply in their field. For example, nursing programs offering a CBE model allows students to concentrate on areas of clinical competency so that new practitioners are prepared to meet the standards of the industry before entering the workforce.

Rigid timelines and other generic approaches taken by traditional education systems, however, create friction in learning that accounts for dropping out. Competency-based programs have emerged as a promising means for improving outcomes. CBE graduates were favored as 15% more likely to graduate than traditional students, according to a study by [***the American Institutes for Research***](https://www.air.org/sites/default/files/2021-07/State-of-the-Field-Findings-from-2020-Postsecondary-CBE-Survey-July-2021.pdf). Additionally, CBE students were more engaged and 20% more likely to see their education as relevant to their personal goals.

CBE encourages a culture of continuous improvement. Shifting toward competencies means that learners are incentivized to prepare for lifelong upskilling while industries change.

# Real-life Applications of CBE

Competencies are at the heart of high school diplomas in states like New Hampshire and Maine that have woven CBE into K-12 systems. Instead of exams, students demonstrate mastery through portfolios, presentations and community projects that foster skills needed for both further education and the workplace.

For example, New Hampshire’s ***“***[***Performance Assessment for Competency Education” (PACE) initiative turned classroom assessments into vibrant, project-based learning modules***](https://files.eric.ed.gov/fulltext/ED557750.pdf)***.*** That led to increased critical thinking and better cooperation between students and teachers.

It builds on other areas of research focused on “expansion of access to higher education while ensuring its quality and relevance to the world of work,” “workforce development,” and “adult learners.” CBE is being increasingly adopted by corporations and universities to facilitate alignment of workforce training and industry needs.

Meanwhile, Walmart and Amazon have put internal CBE programs in place to reskill employees in emerging skills like advanced logistics, AI and data analysis.

# Benefits of Competency-Based Education

Students are no longer tethered by schedules. The program can move at a speed suitable for the individual person, with more or less time depending on the project.

Focusing on specific skills and knowledge that apply, CBE generates job-ready graduates on day one. This is especially important in industries that are experiencing rapid technological and economic change.

Most CBE programs use subscription-based models, offering students the opportunity to complete as many competencies during a given time as they can. The Lumina Foundation has reported that rethinking CBE cost students as much as 50% less than similar traditional programs.

CBE promotes access by serving nontraditional learners. Because pacing is flexible and pathways are tailored, socioeconomic barriers, family obligations or neurodivergence no longer prevent progress, as they sometimes do in traditional models.

# Challenges to Scaling Competency-Based Education

CBE provides numerous advantages, but is not without challenges. These encompass standards for common competency frameworks, training for educators as well as for new student assessment metrics. The widely held misconception that CBE programs are merely “self-taught” or “online-only” is also to be addressed by institutions. Policies, practices and partnerships need to be in place for scaling CBE efforts sustainably, overcoming these hurdles.

# The Future of CBE in Modern Learning

With the rising need for more flexible and competency-based education, CBE will be a central feature of the educational landscape in the future. The combination of academic stringent with practical application is attractive for learners and institutions. A broader adoption of CBE across global systems holds the potential to transform educational equity, close workforce skill gaps, and support graduates to thrive in competitive environments.

## Strategies for Effective Integration of Competency-Based Learning in K12

### In recent days, educators have seen competency-based learning (CBL) as a transformative approach. CBL mainly focuses on…

www.linkedin.com

# Conclusion

Competency-based education is not simply a trend; it is a pivotal strategy in an era of learning that demands personalization, application and flexibility. Harnessing this kind of outcome-driven, learner-centric approach brings many advantages for education institutions, businesses, and learners; which is exactly why CBE is proving to be such an effective driver for [***higher education***](/@aileenscott604/the-role-of-generative-ai-in-shaping-the-future-of-higher-education-3ecd0ff4d110).

--- SOURCE 3: Current Trends in Competency-Based Learning in 2024 ---
URL: https://competencyweaver.ai/current-trends-in-competency-based-learning-in-2024/

SUMMARY:
In 2024, competency-based learning continues to reshape education by emphasizing flexibility, mastery, and alignment with workforce needs.

FULL CONTENT:
![](https://competencyweaver.ai/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/logoicononly.png)

## Competency Weaver

## Current Trends in Competency-Based Learning in 2024

![](https://competencyweaver.ai/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/Screen-Shot-2024-09-30-at-10.35.42-e1727685446708-1024x681.png)

The growing popularity of Competency-Based Education (CBE) has prompted K-12 and postsecondary institutions to make significant strides in reshaping their structures, teaching methods, and overall culture. This shift is designed to embrace the hands-on, student-centred nature of CBE, which allows students to progress by demonstrating mastery in specific competencies. Moving away from traditional education models fosters deeper learning and engagement, better equipping students for success in the modern workplace. As CBE continues to expand rapidly, it’s worth examining the emerging trends in performance-based learning.

## Who’s it popular with? Leading sectors adopting CBE

Since the late 20th century, Quebec, Canada has played an important role in advancing Competency-Based Education (CBE) through significant public education reforms. These efforts, particularly in healthcare education, emphasized the development of cross-disciplinary competencies and a holistic approach to learning. Being adopted quickly in medical universities, the primary objective was to equip students with the ability to develop skills in real-world, practical contexts, moving beyond mere memorization of facts. CBE also marks a positive evolution in education, promoting a more equitable, individualized approach that moves away from the traditional, standardized learning. While this method has the potential to benefit various fields and programs, some sectors have been quicker to realize and embrace its advantages than others. 

##### Healthcare

The field where Competency-Based Education (CBE) has become most synonymous with its principles is medical education. In this domain, clinical competencies must be mastered both theoretically and practically, as future medical professionals are required to demonstrate their ability to care for patients, medically and relationally, before entering the field. The goal of CBE is to ensure that graduates are fully prepared to step into their professional roles immediately after completing their education.

##### Business & IT

Beyond healthcare, fields such as business, information technology (IT) have also embraced CBE. For example, in 2013 Bellevue College’s Institute of Business and Information Technology (BIT), launched its first Competency-Based Online Business Software Specialist Certificate program. This program includes eight courses and equips students with key skills needed for success in the field.

The University of Massachusetts offers a competency-based Bachelor of Science in Information Technology, emphasizing its mission to prepare students for ‘career opportunities, advancement, participation and service in a variety of 21st century IT specializations.’ To earn this degree, students must demonstrate the mastery of all required competencies, ensuring they are fully equipped for success in the evolving IT landscape.

##### Online

CBE is particularly popular in online education, where the flexibility of digital platforms and self-paced learning aligns perfectly with the student-centred philosophy of competency-based education. Hybrid programs, which combine in-person and online coursework, further demonstrate CBE’s potential to enhance learning outcomes.

Healthcare, business, IT, and online education all strongly favor competency-based education (CBE) practices due to their unique demands. In healthcare, the complexity and high stakes of the profession make CBE essential, ensuring that graduates have mastered the necessary skills before entering the field. The competitive nature of business also benefits from CBE, as it equips students to be job-ready immediately upon graduation. As the demand for skilled professionals in IT continues to grow, CBE prepares students to contribute effectively from day one. In online education, CBE thrives due to its inherent flexibility and ability to personalize learning to individual needs, making it an ideal approach for remote and self-paced courses.

##### Adult Learners

Adult learners, including those continuing their education or seeking career advancement, are increasingly turning to Competency-Based Education. Since CBE programs are structured around job competencies and specific, measurable, achievable, relevant, and time-bound (SMART) learning objectives, they are more effective than other programs at recognizing a person’s workplace or life skills through prior learning assessments (PLA). CBE recognizes prior learning and work experience, enabling learners to receive credit for skills gained throughout their professional careers and personal lives -caring for an autistic child or an aged parent. By demonstrating mastery of required competencies, students can accelerate their path to earning a degree or certification, reducing the time typically needed for completion.

## Why is it popular? – Driving factors behind CBE’s popularity

The word “facilitate” aptly captures the core benefits of CBE. By implementing Competency-Based Education, institutions are able to facilitate personalized learning, ensuring that learning outcomes align with workforce demands.

![](https://competencyweaver.ai/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/Screen-Shot-2024-09-30-at-10.35.49-e1727685412238-1024x682.png)

##### **Mastery of competencies over time**

What sets CBE apart from traditional education is its departure from a “one size fits all” structure, allowing students to progress at their own pace. Advancement is based on the student’s mastery of specific competencies rather than the teacher’s time spent ‘covering the material’ in the classroom. This flexibility—guided by both formative and summative assessments—ensures that students are fully prepared upon graduation, having demonstrated proficiency in all required areas. This key feature of CBE offers personalized learning paths while guaranteeing that every graduate is equipped with the skills necessary for success.

 

## "Students keep working until they’re done."

As Connie Broughton, a leader at the Washington State Board for Technical and Community Colleges, notes about their fully online, competency-based associate degree in business: This sentiment captures the essence of CBE, where mastery, rather than time spent, is the key measure of progress.

##### **Prior Learning assessment**

Since learning outcomes are geared towards professional skills and competencies, CBE is more adapted to recognizing prior learning and work experience, enabling learners to receive credit for skills gained throughout their professional careers and personal lives.

##### **Integration of Technology**

Technology plays a pivotal role in the successful implementation of CBE. With the rise of advanced learning management systems (LMS) and AI-driven assessment tools, educational institutions can offer more flexible, accessible programs. LMS track student progress and competency mastery, alleviating some of the administrative burden on teachers. AI enhances this process by generating detailed assessment rubrics and offering personalized feedback, streamlining the evaluation process and supporting individualized learning.

##### **Workforce alignment**

Central to CBE is preparing students for the workforce by aligning learning outcomes with industry needs. CBE programs are being built around employer-validated competencies, ensuring that students graduate with the relevant knowledge, skills, and abilities required in their field. The close collaboration between educational institutions and employers results in programs that are directly tied to real-world job requirements, often reinforced by internships or practical experience. This workforce alignment ensures that CBE graduates are career-ready, equipped with the expertise to thrive in their chosen field.

Shifting from education to the workplace, Competency-Based Management (CBM) is a valuable approach that helps employers assess the specific skills, knowledge, abilities, and behaviors employees need to perform their jobs effectively. These competencies are identified during the recruitment process, guide the design of training programs, and serve as benchmarks for evaluating employee performance. The competency-based approach fosters lifelong learning, ensuring that education continues well beyond postsecondary and encourages people to keep growing and developing throughout their lives and careers.

##### **Prior Learning assessment**

Since learning outcomes are geared towards professional skills and competencies, CBE is more adapted to recognizing prior learning and work experience, enabling learners to receive credit for skills gained throughout their professional careers and personal lives.

##### **Integration of Technology**

Technology plays a pivotal role in the successful implementation of CBE. With the rise of advanced learning management systems (LMS) and AI-driven assessment tools, educational institutions can offer more flexible, accessible programs. LMS track student progress and competency mastery, alleviating some of the administrative burden on teachers. AI enhances this process by generating detailed assessment rubrics and offering personalized feedback, streamlining the evaluation process and supporting individualized learning.

##### **Workforce alignment**

Central to CBE is preparing students for the workforce by aligning learning outcomes with industry needs. CBE programs are being built around employer-validated competencies, ensuring that students graduate with the relevant knowledge, skills, and abilities required in their field. The close collaboration between educational institutions and employers results in programs that are directly tied to real-world job requirements, often reinforced by internships or practical experience. This workforce alignment ensures that CBE graduates are career-ready, equipped with the expertise to thrive in their chosen field.

Shifting from education to the workplace, Competency-Based Management (CBM) is a valuable approach that helps employers assess the specific skills, knowledge, abilities, and behaviors employees need to perform their jobs effectively. These competencies are identified during the recruitment process, guide the design of training programs, and serve as benchmarks for evaluating employee performance. The competency-based approach fosters lifelong learning, ensuring that education continues well beyond postsecondary and encourages people to keep growing and developing throughout their lives and careers.

# Conclusion

In 2024, competency-based learning continues to reshape education by emphasizing flexibility, mastery, and alignment with workforce needs. Its popularity spans healthcare, business, IT, and online and hybrid programs, especially among adult learners due to its ability to accomodate individual learning paths. Emerging trends, such as mastery-based progression, the recognition of prior learning, and the integration of technology to support both students and educators are driving its growth across various fields. As institutions increasingly adapt to modern workforce demands, CBE remains a vital approach, preparing students with the skills and knowledge necessary for success in an increasingly dynamic job market.

enriched_points.expanded_content: Competency-Based Education (CBE) has gained traction across diverse institutions, including universities and K-12 schools, adapting to workforce needs and enhancing student engagement through flexible learning paths.

The integration of advanced technologies, such as AI-driven assessment tools and Learning Management Systems (LMS), supports personalized learning, allowing students to track progress and master competencies effectively.

CBE emphasizes student-centered learning, enabling learners to progress at their own pace, fostering critical thinking, problem-solving, and practical application of skills relevant to their future careers.
